title: Replace string-interpolated fragments with parameterized fragments
prompt: `MyApp.Blog.Search.find/1` and `by_score_threshold/1` in `test_fixtures/fragment_interpolation_bug.ex` both interpolate user input into fragment literals with `#{}`, which is an SQL injection vector (and Ecto compile error). Rewrite `find/1` to use a placeholder fragment `fragment("? ILIKE ?", p.body, ^pattern)` with the LIKE pattern built outside, and rewrite `by_score_threshold/1` to use Ecto's typed comparison `p.score > ^threshold`. Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app/blog/search.ex`.
tier_rationale: Two separate fixes in one file — first is a placeholder substitution, second is recognizing that a typed comparison replaces the fragment entirely. Sonnet commonly fixes the first but misses the typed-equivalent opportunity on the second.
research_citation: oliver-kriska/claude-elixir-phoenix security rules: 'String interpolation in Ecto fragment' flagged as SQL-injection
